original_abstract: This research investigates the strategic internalization of Islamic educational values to foster character development among students at Inpres 2 Wagom Elementary School. Specifically, the study explores the planning, implementation, and evaluation phases of this value-integration process. Employing a qualitative descriptive approach, data were gathered through semi-structured interviews, field observations, and comprehensive documentation, utilizing various audiovisual tools to ensure accuracy. Primary insights were sourced directly from the school principal and teaching faculty. The collected data underwent a rigorous analytical process involving data reduction, systematic display, and verification to draw meaningful conclusions. The findings reveal that the internalization of Islamic principles at SD Inpres 2 Wagom is centered on a holistic assessment of the students, prioritizing the depth of their understanding, the sincerity of their appreciation, and the consistent manifestation of these values in their daily conduct.
humanized_abstract: This study aimed to explore the strategic internalization of Islamic educational values to nurture students’ characters at Inpres 2 Wagom Elementary School. Furthermore, this study examined the phases of planning, implementing, and evaluating the value-integration process. Based on the qualitative descriptive approach, the researcher collected the data by means of semi-structured interviews, observations, and documents. The researcher used audiovisual aids in order to make sure that the data collected were true and reliable. The source of primary data were the school principal and teachers. The researcher analyzed the data collected in several stages, namely data reduction, data display, and verification. The result of this research shows that the internalization of Islamic values at SD Inpres 2 Wagom was based on an overall evaluation of students’ understanding, appreciation, and manifestation of the values they had received. All in all, the values they received were expected to help them become worthy and competent individuals in the modern age.
